name: Suitefish CMS
description: A CMS that doesn’t try to reinvent the wheel—just makes sure the wheel is secure, fast, and doesn’t randomly fall off in production. Built for developers who enjoy flexibility without the usual “why is this even configurable?” moments. Surprisingly opinionated where it matters, and quiet where it should be.

name: Game Development
description: We are crafting horror puzzle games designed to make players question both their logic and their life choices. Expect clever mechanics, unsettling atmospheres, and at least one moment where you confidently solve something… only to realize that was a mistake.

name: Bind9 Web Manager
description: Because editing DNS configs by hand at 3 a.m. is a character-building experience nobody actually wants. This tool wraps Bind9 in a UI that makes complex setups manageable, replication less painful, and user management something you won’t dread explaining to others.

name: Nuke Everything
description: When “delete” and “format” just don’t feel convincing enough. This tool ensures your data is not just gone, but gone with intent. Ideal for those moments when you absolutely, positively do not want anything recoverable—no questions asked, no traces left behind.

name: N-Body Simulator
description: A small JavaScript experiment that quickly turns into a chaotic reminder of why orbital mechanics is hard. Watch bodies dance, collide, or spiral into oblivion—all powered by physics that behaves correctly just often enough to keep things interesting.
